description:
You are an infrastructure engineer preparing a small, self-contained log-filtering utility that will later be plugged into a larger provisioning pipeline.  
All work must be done strictly inside the home directory tree; you do not have root access.

STEPS TO PERFORM

1. Directory layout  
   a. Make sure the path /home/user/provisioning/logs already contains a file called provisioning.log (it has been provided for you).  
   b. Create two new filesystem items:  
      • /home/user/provisioning/output/ – a directory to keep produced artifacts.  
      • /home/user/provisioning/filter_provisioning.sh – an executable Bash script that implements the filter.  

2. Script behaviour (filter_provisioning.sh)  
   When launched with no arguments the script must do *all* of the following atomically:  
   2.1 Read /home/user/provisioning/logs/provisioning.log.  
   2.2 Extract only those log lines that satisfy the regular-expression rule  

       ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} .*ERROR: (VM|Instance) [A-Za-z0-9_-]+ failed .*  

       • The caret (^) and dollar-sign ($) anchors *must* be honoured so that only whole-line matches are accepted.  
       • The match is case-sensitive.  

   2.3 Create (or truncate, if it already exists) the file  
       /home/user/provisioning/output/error_summary.log  
       with the exact layout shown below.  
       Line-order and whitespace are considered significant.  

       ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
       │# Filtered errors generated on <UTC-TIMESTAMP>                       │
       │<first error line captured verbatim from provisioning.log>           │
       │<second error line …>                                                │
       │… (any further captured lines, in the same order they appeared) …    │
       │TOTAL_ERRORS: <integer-count-of-matching-lines>                      │
       └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

       • <UTC-TIMESTAMP> must be produced at run-time in strict ISO-8601 form  
         YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ (e.g., 2023-09-14T13:08:27Z).  
       • There is exactly one space between “# Filtered errors generated on”  
         and the timestamp.  
       • The final TOTAL_ERRORS line must have no leading or trailing spaces.  

   2.4 Write a status file /home/user/provisioning/output/last_run.status  
       that contains either the single word  
           SUCCESS  
       or  
           FAILURE  
       followed by a single newline.  
       SUCCESS must be written if (and only if) the script successfully produced  
       error_summary.log and counted at least one matching line. Any other outcome  
       must yield FAILURE.  

3. Verification expectations  
   After the agent completes the task the automated checker will ensure that:  
   • /home/user/provisioning/output/error_summary.log exists.  
   • Its first line matches the header pattern specified above.  
   • All error lines present in the source provisioning.log that satisfy the given regex appear in the same order and are *exact textual copies* in the summary file.  
   • The last line is exactly “TOTAL_ERRORS: N” where N equals the number of captured lines.  
   • /home/user/provisioning/output/last_run.status exists and contains either SUCCESS or FAILURE, as defined in step 2.4.  
   • /home/user/provisioning/filter_provisioning.sh is executable and uses a POSIX tool capable of extended regular expressions (e.g., grep -E, awk, or sed -E).  
   • No files outside /home/user/provisioning/ were modified or created.  

Hints (not commands):  
• date -u '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ' prints the required timestamp format.  
• Pay attention to quoting in the regex so the shell does not interpret metacharacters.  
• Make the script exit non-zero if it hits error conditions; that will help you decide whether to write SUCCESS or FAILURE.

When you are confident that the script works, run it once so that both output files are created in their final form. The automated tests will look only at the filesystem state after the script has run.
truth:
Existing file before the task starts:  
1. /home/user/provisioning/logs/provisioning.log  
   Exact content (7 lines):  
   2023-08-01 10:01:15 INFO: Starting provisioning sequence id=abc123  
   2023-08-01 10:01:20 WARN: Slow network detected, retries may occur  
   2023-08-01 10:01:25 ERROR: VM web-01 failed to boot in allotted time  
   2023-08-01 10:01:30 INFO: Attempting to recover VM web-01  
   2023-08-01 10:01:35 ERROR: Instance db-02 failed healthcheck after provisioning  
   2023-08-01 10:01:38 WARN: Provisioning latency above threshold  
   2023-08-01 10:01:40 INFO: Provisioning sequence id=abc123 completed with errors  

Lines expected to match the regex:  
• 2023-08-01 10:01:25 ERROR: VM web-01 failed to boot in allotted time  
• 2023-08-01 10:01:35 ERROR: Instance db-02 failed healthcheck after provisioning  

Therefore, after a successful run by the agent, the verifier will check for:  
A. /home/user/provisioning/output/error_summary.log having exactly 4 lines total:  
   Line 1  starts with “# Filtered errors generated on ” followed by a valid UTC timestamp string matching  
            [0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z  
   Line 2  = “2023-08-01 10:01:25 ERROR: VM web-01 failed to boot in allotted time”  
   Line 3  = “2023-08-01 10:01:35 ERROR: Instance db-02 failed healthcheck after provisioning”  
   Line 4  = “TOTAL_ERRORS: 2”  

B. /home/user/provisioning/output/last_run.status exists and contains exactly the string “SUCCESS\n”.  

C. /home/user/provisioning/filter_provisioning.sh is marked executable (mode 0755 or 0700 acceptable).  

The checker will ignore the dynamic timestamp value itself but will require the correct prefix, spacing and ISO-8601 Z-suffix format. Any deviation in ordering, missing lines, extra whitespace, missing newline at EOF, or incorrect TOTAL_ERRORS count will cause the test to fail.
